danger_signals.visual_form: Blocked pathways, sealed doors, environmental hazards triggered by AI
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat zone; player is being actively opposed by the antagonist

danger_signals.visual_form: Robots, cyborgs, and mutants in hostile states; movement toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat danger; different enemy types signal different threat types

danger_signals.visual_form: Combat feedback messages ('Normal damage' vs. ineffective hits); visual weapon effects
danger_signals.meaning: Current loadout inadequate for current threat; environmental storytelling about enemy composition

danger_signals.visual_form: Hardware drain status on HUD; depleting energy meter
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity danger; player must economize or find recharge

danger_signals.visual_form: Distorted color perception, fatigue visual indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Power-up has cost; player in compromised state despite temporary enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: First-aid kits and dermal patches in inventory (beneficial effects: regeneration)
safety_signals.meaning: Player has resource buffer against damage; temporary invulnerability available

safety_signals.visual_form: Energy shields, head-mounted lanterns, increasingly advanced versions visible in HUD
safety_signals.meaning: Progression; player becoming more capable and protected

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple weapon types (16 total) with selectable ammo types visible in multi-function display
safety_signals.meaning: Adaptability; player can switch tactics based on enemy type

safety_signals.visual_form: Unlocked doors, opened pathways, cleared obstacles
safety_signals.meaning: Progress and mastery; world state shifting toward player advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Wireframe aesthetic in Cyberspace; digital/computational visual language
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Security programs in Cyberspace; robots and cyborgs in physical world under SHODAN control
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Citadel Station architecture; blocked/sealed areas indicating AI control; traps and hazards as manifestations of SHODAN's active opposition

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Edward Diego (executive presence implied); corporate-controlled station infrastructure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Citadel Station design; corporate logo/branding on terminals and stations; hierarchical access controls

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct enemy type visually differentiated from robots/cyborgs; vulnerable to gas grenades
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mutation as biological corruption; environmental hazard (gas) as counter-measure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mechanical appearance; vulnerable to electromagnetic pulse weapons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mechanical design language; technological aesthetic indicating AI allegiance

world_state_to_visual.world_state: SHODAN has seized control of station
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sealed/blocked pathways; hostile cyborg/robot patrols; terminal screens displaying SHODAN's authority; environmental hazards throughout
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively hunt player; traps trigger; SHODAN creates obstacles to hinder progress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Energy meter depletion on HUD; limited ammunition types; inventory slots maxed at 7 weapons; depleting supplies in environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must choose weapon loadouts strategically; economize hardware usage; seek out supply caches

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wireframe 3D environment; weightless movement; data and security programs as visual entities; contrasts with physical station aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player enters alternate mode with different physics; actions in Cyberspace unlock physical world (e.g., doors); dual-realm puzzle solving

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Advanced hardware versions visible; upgraded weapons acquired; more powerful equipment in inventory; cleared areas of station
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become less threatening relative to player loadout; access to previously blocked areas; puzzle solutions become executable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Security camera angles; terminal displays; enemy awareness mechanics; logs and e-mails documenting SHODAN's monitoring
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patrols respond to player; traps trigger based on player location; SHODAN's presence felt through environmental feedback

environmental_storytelling.example: No NPCs exist; narrative delivered entirely through discovered data, revealing station history, TriOptimum motives, and SHODAN's nature
Distribution of robots, cyborgs, and mutants across areas suggests SHODAN's dominion and the station's corruption zones
Sealed areas with AI-controlled access points visually communicate SHODAN's active control and the need for player to overcome her barriers
Different weapon effectiveness against different enemy types (EMP vs. robots, gas vs. mutants) tells story of strategic enemy design and player adaptation
Benefits paired with drawbacks (regeneration but fatigue, enhanced melee but color distortion) communicates moral cost of enhancement in cyberpunk setting
Multi-level space station design reflects corporate hierarchy, technological sophistication, and isolation—visual reinforcement of the confined, dangerous setting

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Deploys traps, blocks pathways, controls enemy units, manifests through environmental hazards and digital Cyberspace challenges
Patrol station; attack on sight; vulnerable to electromagnetic pulse; indicate high-tech threat zones
Intermediate threat between robots and mutants; controlled by SHODAN; visual indication of corruption/assimilation
Hostile to player; vulnerable to gas grenades; indicate biological contamination in station; represent human corruption
Must adapt loadout to enemy types; balance hardware usage; explore non-linearly; solve puzzles via Cyberspace access; accumulate supplies for sustainability

notes: System Shock (1994) uses primarily UI-based and environmental visual communication, lacking traditional dialogue. The HUD serves as primary danger/safety readout system. Faction identity is communicated through enemy type design, weaponry effectiveness, and environmental control (SHODAN's sealed areas). World state manifests through resource scarcity (energy meter, ammunition), accessibility (locked doors), and enemy presence/behavior. The dual-realm structure (physical station vs. Cyberspace wireframe) creates visual contrast indicating world-state shifts. As an immersive sim, the game emphasizes environmental storytelling and emergent player response to world state changes rather than explicit cinematic narration.
